pi_items.source: EPA AIM Act allowance consumption caps: 2024-2028 at 60% of baseline = 40% reduction
EPA AIM Act allowance consumption caps: 2029-2033 at 30% of baseline = 70% reduction
EPA AIM Act allowance consumption caps: 2036 and after at 15% of baseline = 85% reduction
EPA Technology Transitions GWP Reference Table: R-410A official GWP is 2088, within range 1900-2200
EPA Technology Transitions GWP Reference Table: R-410A official GWP is 2088, within range 1900-2200
ASHRAE Standard 34 and multiple refrigerant sources confirm R-410A is classified A1
EPA Technology Transitions GWP Reference Table: R-32 official GWP is 675, within range 600-750
EPA Technology Transitions GWP Reference Table: R-32 official GWP is 675, within range 600-750
ASHRAE Standard 34: R-32 is classified as A2L (low toxicity, mildly flammable)
EPA Technology Transitions GWP Reference Table: R-454B official GWP is 465, within range 400-500
EPA Technology Transitions GWP Reference Table: R-454B official GWP is 465, within range 400-500
ASHRAE Standard 34: R-454B is classified as A2L (69.1% R-32 and 31.1% R-1234yf blend)
EPA Technology Transitions GWP Reference Table: R-290 (propane) official GWP is 3.3, within range 0-10
EPA Technology Transitions GWP Reference Table: R-290 (propane) official GWP is 3.3, within range 0-10
ASHRAE Standard 34: R-290 (propane) is classified as A3 (low toxicity, high flammability)
EPA Technology Transitions GWP Reference Table: R-1234yf official GWP is 1, within range 0-5
EPA Technology Transitions GWP Reference Table: R-1234yf official GWP is 1, within range 0-5
ASHRAE Standard 34: R-1234yf is classified as A2L (low toxicity, mildly flammable)
EPA Section 608 regulations require technicians who maintain, service, repair or dispose of equipment containing refrigerants to be certified
EPA Section 608 of Clean Air Act prohibits venting of refrigerants to atmosphere during maintenance, service, repair, or disposal
EPA Section 608 recordkeeping requirements: technicians must keep records of refrigerant transactions for minimum 3 years
AIM Act was signed into law on December 27, 2020 as part of Consolidated Appropriations Act of 2021 (Public Law 116-260)

privileged_info_summary: The EPA AIM Act refrigerant phasedown regulation establishes HFC reduction targets of 40% by 2024, 70% by 2029, and 85% by 2036. The task references four alternative refrigerants to R-410A (GWP 2088, ASHRAE A1): R-32 with GWP 675 and ASHRAE A2L classification, R-454B with GWP 465 and ASHRAE A2L classification, R-290 (propane) with GWP 3.3 and ASHRAE A3 classification, and R-1234yf with GWP 1 and ASHRAE A2L classification. EPA Section 608 regulations require technician certification for all refrigerant handling, prohibit venting of refrigerants to the atmosphere, and mandate 3-year recordkeeping of refrigerant transactions.
